request.freeText: my subscriptions are out of control

response.scoreModifierReason: Nothing in the input warrants a situational adjustment beyond what the scale captures.
response.summary: You own your place in a high-cost state on a solid income, which is genuinely good — but you flagged subscriptions as out of control without naming a single one. That usually means recurring charges are quietly draining you on autopay, unwatched. The fix is visibility, not more income.
response.roast: You typed "subscriptions are out of control" like that's a budget plan. You can't name one charge that's bleeding you — the call is coming from inside the bank app and you're just vibing.
insights: You named the problem but gave zero numbers — the leak is real but invisible until you actually look.
Owning in a high-cost state means your fixed costs are already heavy; autopay creep stings harder here.
Small recurring charges feel harmless solo but stack into a real monthly drain you stop noticing.
The fact you can't quantify it is the whole problem — you can't fix what you won't measure.
On a solid income, this is a visibility issue, not a survival crisis — one audit session fixes it.
topProblems: Recurring subscription spending you can't currently name or total.
No visibility into how much quietly leaves your account on autopay each month.
positiveBehaviors: You own your home in a high-cost state — that's real long-term wealth building.
You flagged the pattern yourself — awareness is genuinely the hardest first step.
Low debt and some savings means you have breathing room to fix this without crisis mode.

topFix.action: Pull your last two bank and card statements, list every recurring charge, and cancel anything you forgot you were paying for — takes one hour.
